item.dc:creator: Angela Yang
item.description: International tourists traveling through the U.S. ahead of FIFA’s festivities have been documenting their American experiences on social media.

media:content.media:text: Elsa Thora, a Swedish citizen visiting the United States during the World Cup.
media:content.media:description: Elsa Thora, a Swedish citizen, has gone viral on X for her posts about visiting the U.S. for the World Cup.

item.title: They came for the World Cup. They fell in love with Buc-ee’s and ranch dip.

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Courtney Kube
Carol E. Lee
item.description: The president is expected to press the leaders to find ways to swiftly increase production of weaponry for the Pentagon, sources told NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Trump Signs Order Directing Customs Crackdown Amid Trade Gaps
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during an executive order signing in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, DC, US, on Wednesday, June 3, 2026. Trump signed an executive order designed to tighten customs enforcement, his latest move to ramp up protectionist trade policies.

media:content.media:text: An image U.S. Central Command shared on X alongside a post about the U.S.' completing strikes in response to Iran’s attack on an Apache helicopter.
media:content.media:description: An image U.S. Central Command shared on X alongside a post about the U.S.' completing strikes in response to Iran’s attack on an Apache helicopter.

item.title: Defense industry leaders to meet with Trump amid missile supply worries, sources say

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Shabad
Sarah Dean
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Thursday that he’s nominating Jay Clayton, the U.S.

media:content.media:text: Jay Clayton.
media:content.media:description: Jay Clayton, US attorney for the Southern District of New York (SDNY), during the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York, US, on Wednesday, June 3, 2026.

item.title: Trump nominates Jay Clayton as director of national intelligence

item.dc:creator: Mosheh Gains
Courtney Kube
Gordon Lubold
Daniel Arkin
item.description: The Pentagon returned to “normal operations” Thursday afternoon after an air quality issue prompted a shelter-in-place order.“Earlier this morning, Pentagon occupants were notified of a potential air quality issue, prompting immediate precautionary safety measures and evaluation,” Pentagon chief spokesman Sean Parnell said in a statement.

media:content.media:text: The Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia.
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia, US, on Wednesday, Oct. 15, 2025.

item.title: Pentagon operating normally after ‘air quality issue’ sparks shelter-in-place order

item.dc:creator: Joelle Gross
Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: The Spurs-Knicks games so far have been decided by an average of 4 points. The average margin across NBA Finals series is 11.8 points.

media:content.media:text: OG Anunoby makes a game-winning tip shot against San Antonio Spurs guards Dylan Harper and Devin Vassell on the basketball court
media:content.media:description: The moment after New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby made a game-winning tip shot against the San Antonio Spurs during Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday. The Knicks won by 1 point, the second 1-point game of the series.

item.title: Data shows just how close these NBA Finals games have been

item.dc:creator: Melanie Zanona
Katherine Doyle
item.description: President Donald Trump has taken political revenge on all four Republicans who signed onto the Epstein files release law.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-GA) speaks at a news conference alongside Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) at the U.S. Capitol Building on May 01, 2024 in Washington, DC.
media:content.media:description: GOP Reps. Marjorie Taylor Greene and Thomas Massie both drew President Donald Trump's ire over the Jeffrey Epstein files and other issues.

item.title: Republicans who pushed Epstein files release see political careers upended by Trump

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Wholesale inflation surged to its highest level since late 2022, as fuel prices pushed higher by the war with Iran ripple across the economy.

media:content.media:text: CORRECTION / US-POLITICS-TRUMP
media:content.media:description: President Donald at John F. Kennedy International Airport, in New York City, on Tuesday.

item.title: The inflation surge is hitting businesses, too

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: One second an officer is seen drawing his weapon from his holster like a gunslinger and pointing it directly in the direction of a dashcam video camera in an approaching police car.

media:content.media:text: Pasadena Police Department.
media:content.media:description: Pasadena Police Department.

item.title: Video shows California cop accidentally shooting fellow cop in ‘horseplay’ incident

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Shabad
Sarah Dean
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Thursday that he’s nominating Jay Clayton, the U.S.

media:content.media:text: Jay Clayton.

item.title: Trump nominates Jay Clayton as director of national intelligence

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
item.description: The New Hampshire Supreme Court on Thursday overturned the murder conviction of a man who was accused of killing his 5-year-old-daughter and moving her body around for months before disposing it.

media:content.media:text: Montgomery was found guilty of second-degree murder earlier in the year in the death of his 5-year-old daughter, Harmony, who police believe was killed nearly two years before she was reported missing in 2021 and whose body was never found.
media:content.media:description: Adam Montgomery arrives for his sentencing hearing at Hillsborough Superior Court, in Manchester, N.H., in 2024.

item.title: Father of Harmony Montgomery has conviction for her murder overturned

item.dc:creator: Melanie Zanona
Katherine Doyle
item.description: President Donald Trump has taken political revenge on all four Republicans who signed onto the Epstein files release law.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-GA) speaks at a news conference alongside Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) at the U.S. Capitol Building on May 01, 2024 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Republicans who pushed Epstein files release see political careers upended by Trump

item.dc:creator: David Hickey
Greg Rosenstein
item.description: The 23rd edition of the men’s World Cup is finally here, with the opening ceremony taking place at Mexico City’s Azteca Stadium today ahead of co-hosts Mexico taking on South Africa.

media:content.media:text: Mexico's forward Raul Jimenez reaches for the ball during friendly match against Serbia in Toluca, Mexico on June 4, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Mexico's forward Raul Jimenez reaches for the ball during friendly match against Serbia in Toluca, Mexico on June 4, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Son Heung-Min of South Korea controls the ball
media:content.media:description: Son Heung-Min of South Korea controls the ball during a match against El Salvador on June 3 in Provo, Utah.

media:content.media:text: Lionel Messi of Argentina runs during a friendly match against Iceland on June 9, 2026 in Auburn, Ala.
media:content.media:description: Lionel Messi of Argentina during a friendly match against Iceland on June 9 in Auburn, Ala.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: The World Cup is finally here

item.description: The Pentagon returned to “normal operations” Thursday afternoon after an air quality issue prompted a shelter-in-place order.“Earlier this morning, Pentagon occupants were notified of a potential air quality issue, prompting immediate precautionary safety measures and evaluation,” Pentagon chief spokesman Sean Parnell said in a statement.

item.title: Pentagon operating normally after ‘air quality issue’ sparks shelter-in-place order

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: A New York judge ruled Thursday that congressional hopeful Brad Lander is not guilty of misdemeanor charges against him related to a September inspection attempt at an immigration holding facility in Manhattan.“I feel genuinely moved today by the rule of law — by the excellent legal counsel I had, by the thoughtful consideration by the judge, and by the simple knowledge that I could enter and leave the court safely,” Lander, a close political ally of Mayor Zohran Mamdani, said at a news co

media:content.media:text: US Congressional Candidate Brad Lander Court Hearing Over ICE Charges
media:content.media:description: Brad Lander, former New York City comptroller, arrives at federal court in New York on Wednesday.

item.title: Brad Lander found not guilty of charges in incident at NYC immigration detention center

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Dangerous heat and humidity are bearing down on the Northeast on Thursday, following a surge of more than 350 storms on Wednesday — one of the stormiest days of 2026 so far

media:content.media:text: Michigan Extreme Weather
media:content.media:description: Storm damage in Freeland, Mich., on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: chairs flying into oncoming traffic during windy day in Chicago
media:content.media:description: Storm damage in Freeland, Mich., on Tuesday.

item.title: Severe thunderstorms and humid weather rip through the Northeast and Midwest

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: European Union regulators are studying Paramount Skydance’s prospective takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery

media:content.media:text: Warner Bros. Studios in Burbank, Calif.; Paramount Pictures lot in Los Angeles.
media:content.media:description: Warner Bros. Studios in Burbank, Calif.; Paramount Pictures lot in Los Angeles.

item.title: E.U. reviewing Paramount-Warner merger over Middle Eastern wealth funds’ backing

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
item.description: Florida’s newly drawn congressional lines will be used during the 2026 midterm elections, after the state Supreme Court rejected an attempt by outside groups to block the new Republican-drawn plan

media:content.media:text: Key Speakers At Boom Belt Event
media:content.media:description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis.

item.title: Florida Supreme Court rejects challenge to new GOP-drawn congressional maps

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins
Minyvonne Burke
item.description: Vance Boelter, the Minnesota man accused of impersonating a law enforcement officer and fatally shooting the state’s former house speaker in what authorities have described as a politically motivated assassination, plead guilty Thursday

media:content.media:text: A photograph of Melissa Hortman and her husband amongst flowers
media:content.media:description: A photograph of Sen. Melissa Hortman, D-Minn., and her husband at memorial outside the Minnesota State Capitol, in St. Paul, Minn., on June 15, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Vance Boelter is arrested
media:content.media:description: Vance Boelter during his arrest on June 15, 2025.

item.title: Man accused of killing Minnesota lawmaker and her husband pleads guilty

item.dc:creator: Phil Helsel
item.description: The UFC matches to be held on the lawn of the White House on President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday are sure to be a spectacle unseen in the history of the storied complex

media:content.media:text: Hooverball Game on White House Lawn in 1933.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: The UFC arena on the South Lawn of the White House during a media preview on Thursday, June 11, 2026.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: President Gerald Ford, Chief of Staff Donald Rumsfeld, and David Kennerly, Personal Photographer to the President, following a Tennis Match on the White House Tennis Courts in 1975.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: President Barack Obama shoots hoops on the White House Basketball Court during a break between meetings, May 7, 2010.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: President Bush and John Denver pitch horseshoes in 1989.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: Truman and Vets Bowling
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: President Richard Nixon Bowling in the White House Bowling Alley in 1971.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

media:content.media:text: Calvin Coolidge's mechanical horse.
media:content.media:description: A Hoover-ball game on White House lawn in 1933.

item.title: Trump’s UFC matches are unprecedented, but sports at the White House aren’t

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: The search for a 5-year-old girl who was swept out to sea in Laguna Beach, California, has been suspended, the U.S.

media:content.media:text: California High Surf Missing Girl

item.title: Search suspended for 5-year-old girl swept out to sea in California’s Laguna Beach

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: With the U.S. hosting men’s World Cup games for the first time since 1994, the attention is on whether the U.S. can go far under coach Mauricio Pochettino.

media:content.media:text: United States Training & Media Availability - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Tim Ream #13 and Sebastian Berhalter #14 participate during a training session ahead of the 2026 World Cup at the Great Park Sports Complex on June 9, 2026 in Irvine, Calif.

media:content.media:text: United States Training & Media Availability - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Chris Brady, Gio Reyna, Antonee Robinson and Joe Scally at Community Day in Irvine on Tuesday,

media:content.media:text: United States Training & Media Availability - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino at a training session Wednesday.

item.title: As World Cup opener nears, U.S. men know they’ll play under a spotlight

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Minutes after the New York Knicks had won Game 4 of the NBA Finals, Taylor Swift led an impromptu pep rally in the bowels of Madison Square Garden, on her way out.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - Game Four
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama looks on during the second-half collapse against the New York Knicks in Game 4 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - San Antonio Spurs v New York Knicks
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama looks on during the second-half collapse against the New York Knicks in Game 4 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: BASKET-NBA-FINALS-GAME 4-FANS
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama looks on during the second-half collapse against the New York Knicks in Game 4 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden on Wednesday.

item.title: Silence fills Spurs locker room after devastating Game 4 meltdown
